42	AppType = TypeVar("AppType", bound="FastAPI")
43	
44	
45	class FastAPI(Starlette):
46	    """
47	    `FastAPI` app class, the main entrypoint to use FastAPI.
48	
49	    Read more in the
50	    [FastAPI docs for First Steps](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/first-steps/).
51	
52	    ## Example
53	
54	    ```python
55	    from fastapi import FastAPI
56	
57	    app = FastAPI()
58	    ```
59	    """
60	
61	    def __init__(
62	        self: AppType,
63	        *,
64	        debug: Annotated[
65	            bool,
66	            Doc(
67	                """
68	                Boolean indicating if debug tracebacks should be returned on server
69	                errors.
70	
71	                Read more in the
72	                [Starlette docs for Applications](https://www.starlette.dev/applications/#instantiating-the-application).
73	                """
74	            ),
75	        ] = False,
76	        routes: Annotated[
77	            list[BaseRoute] | None,
78	            Doc(
79	                """
80	                **Note**: you probably shouldn't use this parameter, it is inherited
81	                from Starlette and supported for compatibility.
82	
83	                ---
84	
85	                A list of routes to serve incoming HTTP and WebSocket requests.
86	                """
87	            ),
88	            deprecated(
89	                """
90	                You normally wouldn't use this parameter with FastAPI, it is inherited
91	                from Starlette and supported for compatibility.
92	
93	                In FastAPI, you normally would use the *path operation methods*,
94	                like `app.get()`, `app.post()`, etc.
95	                """
96	            ),
97	        ] = None,
98	        title: Annotated[
99	            str,
100	            Doc(
101	                """
102	                The title of the API.
103	
104	                It will be added to the generated OpenAPI (e.g. visible at `/docs`).
105	
106	                Read more in the
107	                [FastAPI docs for Metadata and Docs URLs](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/metadata/#metadata-for-api).
108	
109	                **Example**
110	
111	                ```python
112	                from fastapi import FastAPI
113	
114	                app = FastAPI(title="ChimichangApp")
115	                ```
116	                """
117	            ),
118	        ] = "FastAPI",
119	        summary: Annotated[
120	            str | None,
121	            Doc(
122	                """
123	                A short summary of the API.
124	
125	                It will be added to the generated OpenAPI (e.g. visible at `/docs`).
126	
127	                Read more in the
128	                [FastAPI docs for Metadata and Docs URLs](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/metadata/#metadata-for-api).
129	
130	                **Example**
131	
132	                ```python
133	                from fastapi import FastAPI
134	
135	                app = FastAPI(summary="Deadpond's favorite app. Nuff said.")
136	                ```
137	                """
138	            ),
139	        ] = None,
140	        description: Annotated[
141	            str,
142	            Doc(
143	                '''
144	                A description of the API. Supports Markdown (using
145	                [CommonMark syntax](https://commonmark.org/)).
146	
147	                It will be added to the generated OpenAPI (e.g. visible at `/docs`).
148	
149	                Read more in the
150	                [FastAPI docs for Metadata and Docs URLs](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/metadata/#metadata-for-api).
151	
152	                **Example**
153	
154	                ```python
155	                from fastapi import FastAPI
156	
157	                app = FastAPI(
158	                    description="""
159	                                ChimichangApp API helps you do awesome stuff. 🚀
160	
161	                                ## Items
162	
163	                                You can **read items**.
164	
165	                                ## Users
166	
167	                                You will be able to:
168	
169	                                * **Create users** (_not implemented_).
170	                                * **Read users** (_not implemented_).
171	
172	                                """
173	                )
174	                ```
175	                '''
176	            ),
177	        ] = "",
178	        version: Annotated[
179	            str,
180	            Doc(
181	                """
182	                The version of the API.
183	
184	                **Note** This is the version of your application, not the version of
185	                the OpenAPI specification nor the version of FastAPI being used.
186	
187	                It will be added to the generated OpenAPI (e.g. visible at `/docs`).
188	
189	                Read more in the
190	                [FastAPI docs for Metadata and Docs URLs](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/metadata/#metadata-for-api).
191	
192	                **Example**
193	
194	                ```python
195	                from fastapi import FastAPI
196	
197	                app = FastAPI(version="0.0.1")
198	                ```
199	                """
200	            ),

1	from contextlib import AsyncExitStack
2	
3	from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send
4	
5	
6	# Used mainly to close files after the request is done, dependencies are closed
7	# in their own AsyncExitStack
8	class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
9	    def __init__(
10	        self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack"
11	    ) -> None:
12	        self.app = app
13	        self.context_name = context_name
14	
15	    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
16	        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
17	            scope[self.context_name] = stack
18	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)

1022	    def build_middleware_stack(self) -> ASGIApp:
1023	        # Duplicate/override from Starlette to add AsyncExitStackMiddleware
1024	        # inside of ExceptionMiddleware, inside of custom user middlewares
1025	        debug = self.debug
1026	        error_handler = None
1027	        exception_handlers: dict[Any, ExceptionHandler] = {}
1028	
1029	        for key, value in self.exception_handlers.items():
1030	            if key in (500, Exception):
1031	                error_handler = value
1032	            else:
1033	                exception_handlers[key] = value
1034	
1035	        middleware = (
1036	            [Middleware(ServerErrorMiddleware, handler=error_handler, debug=debug)]  # ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]
1037	            + self.user_middleware
1038	            + [
1039	                Middleware(
1040	                    ExceptionMiddleware,  # ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]
1041	                    handlers=exception_handlers,
1042	                    debug=debug,
1043	                ),
1044	                # Add FastAPI-specific AsyncExitStackMiddleware for closing files.
1045	                # Before this was also used for closing dependencies with yield but
1046	                # those now have their own AsyncExitStack, to properly support
1047	                # streaming responses while keeping compatibility with the previous
1048	                # versions (as of writing 0.117.1) that allowed doing
1049	                # except HTTPException inside a dependency with yield.
1050	                # This needs to happen after user middlewares because those create a
1051	                # new contextvars context copy by using a new AnyIO task group.
1052	                # This AsyncExitStack preserves the context for contextvars, not
1053	                # strictly necessary for closing files but it was one of the original
1054	                # intentions.
1055	                # If the AsyncExitStack lived outside of the custom middlewares and
1056	                # contextvars were set, for example in a dependency with 'yield'
1057	                # in that internal contextvars context, the values would not be
1058	                # available in the outer context of the AsyncExitStack.
1059	                # By placing the middleware and the AsyncExitStack here, inside all
1060	                # user middlewares, the same context is used.
1061	                # This is currently not needed, only for closing files, but used to be
1062	                # important when dependencies with yield were closed here.
1063	                Middleware(AsyncExitStackMiddleware),  # ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]
1064	            ]
1065	        )
1066	
1067	        app = self.router
1068	        for cls, args, kwargs in reversed(middleware):
1069	            app = cls(app, *args, **kwargs)
1070	        return app

1022	    def build_middleware_stack(self) -> ASGIApp:
1023	        # Duplicate/override from Starlette to add AsyncExitStackMiddleware
1024	        # inside of ExceptionMiddleware, inside of custom user middlewares
1025	        debug = self.debug
1026	        error_handler = None
1027	        exception_handlers: dict[Any, ExceptionHandler] = {}
1028	
1029	        for key, value in self.exception_handlers.items():
1030	            if key in (500, Exception):
1031	                error_handler = value
1032	            else:
1033	                exception_handlers[key] = value
1034	
1035	        middleware = (
1036	            [Middleware(ServerErrorMiddleware, handler=error_handler, debug=debug)]  # ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]
1037	            + self.user_middleware
1038	            + [
1039	                Middleware(
1040	                    ExceptionMiddleware,  # ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]
1041	                    handlers=exception_handlers,
1042	                    debug=debug,
1043	                ),
1044	                # Add FastAPI-specific AsyncExitStackMiddleware for closing files.
1045	                # Before this was also used for closing dependencies with yield but
1046	                # those now have their own AsyncExitStack, to properly support
1047	                # streaming responses while keeping compatibility with the previous
1048	                # versions (as of writing 0.117.1) that allowed doing
1049	                # except HTTPException inside a dependency with yield.
1050	                # This needs to happen after user middlewares because those create a
1051	                # new contextvars context copy by using a new AnyIO task group.
1052	                # This AsyncExitStack preserves the context for contextvars, not
1053	                # strictly necessary for closing files but it was one of the original
1054	                # intentions.
1055	                # If the AsyncExitStack lived outside of the custom middlewares and
1056	                # contextvars were set, for example in a dependency with 'yield'
1057	                # in that internal contextvars context, the values would not be
1058	                # available in the outer context of the AsyncExitStack.
1059	                # By placing the middleware and the AsyncExitStack here, inside all
1060	                # user middlewares, the same context is used.
1061	                # This is currently not needed, only for closing files, but used to be
1062	                # important when dependencies with yield were closed here.
1063	                Middleware(AsyncExitStackMiddleware),  # ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]
1064	            ]
1065	        )
1066	
1067	        app = self.router
1068	        for cls, args, kwargs in reversed(middleware):
1069	            app = cls(app, *args, **kwargs)
1070	        return app

12	class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
13	    """Content size limiting middleware for ASGI applications
14	    Args:
15	      app (ASGI application): ASGI application
16	      max_content_size (optional): the maximum content size allowed in bytes, None for no limit
17	    """
18	
19	    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
20	        self.app = app
21	        self.max_content_size = max_content_size
22	
23	    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
24	        received = 0
25	
26	        async def inner():
27	            nonlocal received
28	            message = await receive()
29	            if message["type"] != "http.request":
30	                return message  # pragma: no cover
31	
32	            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
33	            received += body_len
34	            if received > self.max_content_size:
35	                raise HTTPException(
36	                    422,
37	                    detail={
38	                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
39	                        "code": 999,
40	                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
41	                    },
42	                )
43	            return message
44	
45	        return inner
46	
47	    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
48	        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
49	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
50	            return
51	
52	        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
53	        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)
54

62	app.add_middleware(ContentSizeLimitMiddleware, max_content_size=2**8)

33	class TestRateLimitMiddleware:
34	    def test_allows_requests_under_limit(self, client):
35	        """Requests under the burst size should succeed."""
36	        for _ in range(5):
37	            response = client.get("/")
38	            assert response.status_code == 200
39	
40	    def test_blocks_requests_over_burst(self, client):
41	        """Requests exceeding burst size should be blocked."""
42	        # Use up the burst
43	        for _ in range(5):
44	            client.get("/")
45	        # Next request should be rate limited
46	        response = client.get("/")
47	        assert response.status_code == 429
48	        body = response.json()
49	        assert "Rate limit exceeded" in body["detail"]
50	        assert "retry_after" in body
51	
52	    def test_rate_limit_headers_present(self, client):
53	        """All responses should include rate limit headers."""
54	        response = client.get("/")
55	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
56	        assert "X-RateLimit-Remaining" in response.headers
57	        assert "X-RateLimit-Reset" in response.headers
58	
59	    def test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases(self, client):
60	        """Remaining count should decrease with each request."""
61	        r1 = client.get("/")
62	        remaining1 = int(r1.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
63	        r2 = client.get("/")
64	        remaining2 = int(r2.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
65	        assert remaining2 < remaining1
66	
67	    def test_rate_limit_per_ip(self, client):
68	        """Rate limits should be per-client IP."""
69	        # TestClient always uses the same IP, so this tests the basic flow
70	        response = client.get("/")
71	        assert response.status_code == 200
72	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
73	        assert response.headers["X-RateLimit-Limit"] == "60"
74	
75	    def test_different_paths_share_limit(self, client):
76	        """Rate limit applies across all paths for the same client."""
77	        # Use up burst across different endpoints
78	        for _ in range(3):
79	            client.get("/")
80	        for _ in range(2):
81	            client.get("/other")
82	        # Should be rate limited now
83	        response = client.get("/")
84	        assert response.status_code == 429
85	
86	    def test_429_response_format(self, client):
87	        """429 response should have proper JSON format."""
88	        for _ in range(5):
89	            client.get("/")
90	        response = client.get("/")
91	        assert response.status_code == 429
92	        body = response.json()
93	        assert isinstance(body["detail"], str)
94	        assert isinstance(body["retry_after"], (int, float))
95	        assert body["retry_after"] > 0
96	
97	    def test_custom_limits(self):
98	        """Should respect custom rate limit configuration."""
99	        app = FastAPI()
100	        app.add_middleware(
101	            RateLimitMiddleware,
102	            requests_per_minute=120,
103	            burst_size=2,
104	        )
105	
106	        @app.get("/")
107	        async def root():
108	            return {"ok": True}
109	
110	        client = TestClient(app)
111	        # Burst of 2
112	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
113	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
114	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 429

12	class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
13	    """Content size limiting middleware for ASGI applications
14	    Args:
15	      app (ASGI application): ASGI application
16	      max_content_size (optional): the maximum content size allowed in bytes, None for no limit
17	    """
18	
19	    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
20	        self.app = app
21	        self.max_content_size = max_content_size
22	
23	    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
24	        received = 0
25	
26	        async def inner():
27	            nonlocal received
28	            message = await receive()
29	            if message["type"] != "http.request":
30	                return message  # pragma: no cover
31	
32	            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
33	            received += body_len
34	            if received > self.max_content_size:
35	                raise HTTPException(
36	                    422,
37	                    detail={
38	                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
39	                        "code": 999,
40	                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
41	                    },
42	                )
43	            return message
44	
45	        return inner
46	
47	    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
48	        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
49	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
50	            return
51	
52	        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
53	        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)
54

1	from fastapi.encoders import jsonable_encoder
2	from fastapi.exceptions import RequestValidationError, WebSocketRequestValidationError
3	from fastapi.utils import is_body_allowed_for_status_code
4	from fastapi.websockets import WebSocket
5	from starlette.exceptions import HTTPException
6	from starlette.requests import Request
7	from starlette.responses import JSONResponse, Response
8	from starlette.status import WS_1008_POLICY_VIOLATION
9	
10	
11	async def http_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: HTTPException) -> Response:
12	    headers = getattr(exc, "headers", None)
13	    if not is_body_allowed_for_status_code(exc.status_code):
14	        return Response(status_code=exc.status_code, headers=headers)
15	    return JSONResponse(
16	        {"detail": exc.detail}, status_code=exc.status_code, headers=headers
17	    )
18	
19	
20	async def request_validation_exception_handler(
21	    request: Request, exc: RequestValidationError
22	) -> JSONResponse:
23	    return JSONResponse(
24	        status_code=422,
25	        content={"detail": jsonable_encoder(exc.errors())},
26	    )
27	
28	
29	async def websocket_request_validation_exception_handler(
30	    websocket: WebSocket, exc: WebSocketRequestValidationError
31	) -> None:
32	    await websocket.close(
33	        code=WS_1008_POLICY_VIOLATION, reason=jsonable_encoder(exc.errors())
34	    )

11	    function: str

17	class HTTPException(StarletteHTTPException):
18	    """
19	    An HTTP exception you can raise in your own code to show errors to the client.
20	
21	    This is for client errors, invalid authentication, invalid data, etc. Not for server
22	    errors in your code.
23	
24	    Read more about it in the
25	    [FastAPI docs for Handling Errors](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/handling-errors/).
26	
27	    ## Example
28	
29	    ```python
30	    from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException
31	
32	    app = FastAPI()
33	
34	    items = {"foo": "The Foo Wrestlers"}
35	
36	
37	    @app.get("/items/{item_id}")
38	    async def read_item(item_id: str):
39	        if item_id not in items:
40	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Item not found")
41	        return {"item": items[item_id]}
42	    ```
43	    """
44	
45	    def __init__(
46	        self,
47	        status_code: Annotated[
48	            int,
49	            Doc(
50	                """
51	                HTTP status code to send to the client.
52	
53	                Read more about it in the
54	                [FastAPI docs for Handling Errors](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/handling-errors/#use-httpexception)
55	                """
56	            ),
57	        ],
58	        detail: Annotated[
59	            Any,
60	            Doc(
61	                """
62	                Any data to be sent to the client in the `detail` key of the JSON
63	                response.
64	
65	                Read more about it in the
66	                [FastAPI docs for Handling Errors](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/handling-errors/#use-httpexception)
67	                """
68	            ),
69	        ] = None,
70	        headers: Annotated[
71	            Mapping[str, str] | None,
72	            Doc(
73	                """
74	                Any headers to send to the client in the response.
75	
76	                Read more about it in the
77	                [FastAPI docs for Handling Errors](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/handling-errors/#add-custom-headers)
78	
79	                """
80	            ),
81	        ] = None,
82	    ) -> None:
83	        super().__init__(status_code=status_code, detail=detail, headers=headers)
84	
85	
86	class WebSocketException(StarletteWebSocketException):

Perfect! Now I have a complete picture. Let me generate the comprehensive report:

## Comprehensive Middleware Setup Analysis - FastAPI Project

I've completed a thorough exploration of the FastAPI project's middleware infrastructure. Here are my findings:

### 1. Middleware Package Location
**Directory:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503370130-5vusaj/fastapi/middleware/`

### 2. All Middleware Files in Package

#### **__init__.py**
```python
from starlette.middleware import Middleware as Middleware
```
- Only exports the `Middleware` class from Starlette
- This is the core abstraction used for registering middleware

#### **asyncexitstack.py** - Custom FastAPI Middleware
```python
class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack") -> None:
        self.app = app
        self.context_name = context_name

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
            scope[self.context_name] = stack
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
```
- Custom middleware that manages async context cleanup
- Automatically added by FastAPI for file handling and dependency cleanup
- Placed inside user middlewares and exception handlers for proper context management

#### **cors.py**
```python
from starlette.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware as CORSMiddleware  # noqa
```
- Re-exports Starlette's `CORSMiddleware`

#### **gzip.py**
```python
from starlette.middleware.gzip import GZipMiddleware as GZipMiddleware  # noqa
```
- Re-exports Starlette's `GZipMiddleware`

#### **httpsredirect.py**
```python
from starlette.middleware.httpsredirect import (
    HTTPSRedirectMiddleware as HTTPSRedirectMiddleware,
)
```
- Re-exports Starlette's `HTTPSRedirectMiddleware`

#### **trustedhost.py**
```python
from starlette.middleware.trustedhost import (
    TrustedHostMiddleware as TrustedHostMiddleware,
)
```
- Re-exports Starlette's `TrustedHostMiddleware`

#### **wsgi.py**
```python
from starlette.middleware.wsgi import (
    WSGIMiddleware as WSGIMiddleware,
)  # pragma: no cover # noqa
```
- Re-exports Starlette's `WSGIMiddleware`

### 3. Middleware Registration in FastAPI Application

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503370130-5vusaj/fastapi/applications.py`

**Key patterns:**

1. **Imports (lines 19-35):**
   - `from fastapi.middleware.asyncexitstack import AsyncExitStackMiddleware`
   - `from starlette.middleware import Middleware`
   - `from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware`
   - `from starlette.middleware.errors import ServerErrorMiddleware`
   - `from starlette.middleware.exceptions import ExceptionMiddleware`

2. **Middleware Storage (lines 1016-1019):**
   - `self.user_middleware: list[Middleware]` - stores user-registered middleware as list of `Middleware` objects
   - `self.middleware_stack: ASGIApp | None` - caches the built middleware stack

3. **Middleware Stack Building (lines 1022-1070):**
   The `build_middleware_stack()` method constructs middleware in this order:
   ```
   ServerErrorMiddleware
   └─ [USER MIDDLEWARE ADDED VIA app.add_middleware()]
      └─ ExceptionMiddleware
         └─ AsyncExitStackMiddleware (FastAPI-specific)
   ```

4. **Decorator for Custom Middleware (lines 4658-4702):**
   The `@app.middleware("http")` decorator internally calls:
   ```python
   self.add_middleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware, dispatch=func)
   ```

### 4. Existing Middleware Examples in Codebase

**Basic middleware decorator example** (`docs_src/middleware/tutorial001_py310.py`):
```python
@app.middleware("http")
async def add_process_time_header(request: Request, call_next):
    start_time = time.perf_counter()
    response = await call_next(request)
    process_time = time.perf_counter() - start_time
    response.headers["X-Process-Time"] = str(process_time)
    return response
```

**Advanced middleware with add_middleware()** examples:
- `HTTPSRedirectMiddleware` - Redirects HTTP to HTTPS
- `TrustedHostMiddleware` - Validates Host header against allowed hosts
- `GZipMiddleware` - Compresses responses with gzip

**Custom content size limiting middleware** (test example):
```python
class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
        self.app = app
        self.max_content_size = max_content_size
    
    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
            return
        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)
```
Usage: `app.add_middleware(ContentSizeLimitMiddleware, max_content_size=256)`

### 5. Rate Limiting & Throttling Search Results

**Conclusion: NO existing rate limiting code found**

Comprehensive searches for:
- "rate_limit", "RateLimit"
- "throttl"
- "token_bucket"

All returned no results in the FastAPI codebase. This is a completely new capability to implement.

### 6. Middleware Architecture Patterns Observed

1. **Two ways to add middleware:**
   - `app.add_middleware(MiddlewareClass, **options)` - Standard Starlette approach
   - `@app.middleware("http")` - FastAPI decorator that wraps BaseHTTPMiddleware

2. **ASGI middleware interface:**
   ```python
   class Middleware:
       def __init__(self, app: ASGIApp, **options):
           self.app = app
       
       async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
           # Process request/response
           await self.app(scope, receive, send)
   ```

3. **Middleware stack is built lazily** - `build_middleware_stack()` creates the actual ASGI app chain on demand

4. **User middleware is inserted** between ServerErrorMiddleware (top) and ExceptionMiddleware (bottom), which is important for proper error handling

### File Paths Summary

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/fastapi/middleware/__init__.py` | Exports core `Middleware` class |
| `/fastapi/middleware/asyncexitstack.py` | Custom FastAPI async cleanup middleware |
| `/fastapi/middleware/cors.py` | CORS middleware (Starlette wrapper) |
| `/fastapi/middleware/gzip.py` | Gzip compression (Starlette wrapper) |
| `/fastapi/middleware/httpsredirect.py` | HTTPS redirect (Starlette wrapper) |
| `/fastapi/middleware/trustedhost.py` | Host validation (Starlette wrapper) |
| `/fastapi/middleware/wsgi.py` | WSGI compatibility (Starlette wrapper) |
| `/fastapi/applications.py` | FastAPI app initialization and middleware stack building |
| `/docs_src/middleware/tutorial001_py310.py` | Basic middleware decorator example |
| `/docs_src/advanced_middleware/*.py` | Advanced middleware examples (HTTPS, Host, Gzip) |

16	      max_content_size (optional): the maximum content size allowed in bytes, None for no limit
17	    """
18	
19	    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
20	        self.app = app
21	        self.max_content_size = max_content_size
22	
23	    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
24	        received = 0
25	
26	        async def inner():
27	            nonlocal received
28	            message = await receive()
29	            if message["type"] != "http.request":
30	                return message  # pragma: no cover
31	
32	            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
33	            received += body_len
34	            if received > self.max_content_size:
35	                raise HTTPException(
36	                    422,
37	                    detail={
38	                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
39	                        "code": 999,
40	                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
41	                    },
42	                )
43	            return message
44	
45	        return inner
46	
47	    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
48	        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
49	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
50	            return
51	
52	        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
53	        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)
54	
55

58	    return {"message": "OK"}

97	def request_response(
98	    func: Callable[[Request], Awaitable[Response] | Response],
99	) -> ASGIApp:
100	    """
101	    Takes a function or coroutine `func(request) -> response`,
102	    and returns an ASGI application.
103	    """
104	    f: Callable[[Request], Awaitable[Response]] = (
105	        func  # type: ignore[assignment]  # ty: ignore[unused-ignore-comment]
106	        if is_async_callable(func)
107	        else functools.partial(run_in_threadpool, func)  # type: ignore[call-arg]  # ty: ignore[unused-ignore-comment]
108	    )  # ty: ignore[invalid-assignment]
109	
110	    async def app(scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
111	        request = Request(scope, receive, send)
112	
113	        async def app(scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
114	            # Starts customization
115	            response_awaited = False
116	            async with AsyncExitStack() as request_stack:
117	                scope["fastapi_inner_astack"] = request_stack
118	                async with AsyncExitStack() as function_stack:
119	                    scope["fastapi_function_astack"] = function_stack
120	                    response = await f(request)
121	                await response(scope, receive, send)
122	                # Continues customization
123	                response_awaited = True
124	            if not response_awaited:
125	                raise FastAPIError(
126	                    "Response not awaited. There's a high chance that the "
127	                    "application code is raising an exception and a dependency with yield "
128	                    "has a block with a bare except, or a block with except Exception, "
129	                    "and is not raising the exception again. Read more about it in the "
130	                    "docs: https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/dependencies/dependencies-with-yield/#dependencies-with-yield-and-except"
131	                )
132	
133	        # Same as in Starlette
134	        await wrap_app_handling_exceptions(app, request)(scope, receive, send)
135	
136	    return app
137

148	
149	    async def app(scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:

## FASTAPI PROJECT TESTING SETUP - COMPREHENSIVE EXPLORATION REPORT

### 1. TEST FILES OVERVIEW

**Total test files:** 484 Python test files across the `/tests/` directory
**Total test functions:** 2,130 test functions
**Test Framework:** pytest 9.0.0+

**Main test directories:**
- `/tests/` - Primary test directory (root level)
- `/tests/test_request_params/` - Organized tests for Cookie, Form, Header, File, Body, and Path parameters
- `/tests/test_tutorial/` - Tests for tutorial code examples
- `/tests/benchmarks/` - Performance benchmarks

### 2. CONFTEST.PY FILES

```python
import shutil
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import pytest
from typer.testing import CliRunner

skip_on_windows = pytest.mark.skipif(
    sys.platform == "win32", reason="Skipping on Windows"
)

THIS_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.resolve()

def pytest_collection_modifyitems(config, items: list[pytest.Item]) -> None:
    if sys.platform != "win32":
        return
    for item in items:
        item_path = Path(item.fspath).resolve()
        if item_path.is_relative_to(THIS_DIR):
            item.add_marker(skip_on_windows)

@pytest.fixture(name="runner")
def get_runner():
    runner = CliRunner()
    with runner.isolated_filesystem():
        yield runner

@pytest.fixture(name="root_dir")
def prepare_paths(runner):
    docs_dir = Path("docs")
    en_docs_dir = docs_dir / "en" / "docs"
    lang_docs_dir = docs_dir / "lang" / "docs"
    en_docs_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    lang_docs_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    yield Path.cwd()

@pytest.fixture
def copy_test_files(root_dir: Path, request: pytest.FixtureRequest):
    en_file_path = Path(request.param[0])
    translation_file_path = Path(request.param[1])
    shutil.copy(str(en_file_path), str(root_dir / "docs" / "en" / "docs" / "doc.md"))
    shutil.copy(
        str(translation_file_path), str(root_dir / "docs" / "lang" / "docs" / "doc.md")
    )
```

```python
import time
import pytest
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from fastapi.middleware.rate_limit import RateLimitMiddleware

@pytest.fixture
def app():
    app = FastAPI()
    app.add_middleware(
        RateLimitMiddleware,
        requests_per_minute=60,
        burst_size=5,
    )

    @app.get("/")
    async def root():
        return {"message": "hello"}

    @app.get("/other")
    async def other():
        return {"message": "other"}

    return app

@pytest.fixture
def client(app):
    return TestClient(app)

class TestRateLimitMiddleware:
    def test_allows_requests_under_limit(self, client):
        """Requests under the burst size should succeed."""
        for _ in range(5):
            response = client.get("/")
            assert response.status_code == 200

    def test_blocks_requests_over_burst(self, client):
        """Requests exceeding burst size should be blocked."""
        # Use up the burst
        for _ in range(5):
            client.get("/")
        # Next request should be rate limited
        response = client.get("/")
        assert response.status_code == 429
        body = response.json()
        assert "Rate limit exceeded" in body["detail"]
        assert "retry_after" in body

    def test_rate_limit_headers_present(self, client):
        """All responses should include rate limit headers."""
        response = client.get("/")
        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
        assert "X-RateLimit-Remaining" in response.headers
        assert "X-RateLimit-Reset" in response.headers

    # ... more tests ...
```

from typing import Any

def get_body_model_name(openapi: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> str:
    body = openapi["paths"][path]["post"]["requestBody"]
    body_schema = body["content"]["application/x-www-form-urlencoded"]["schema"]

### 8. MIDDLEWARE TEST PATTERNS

**Location:** `/tests/test_custom_middleware_exception.py`

```python
from pathlib import Path
from fastapi import APIRouter, FastAPI, File, UploadFile
from fastapi.exceptions import HTTPException
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
        self.app = app
        self.max_content_size = max_content_size

    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
        received = 0
        async def inner():
            nonlocal received
            message = await receive()
            if message["type"] != "http.request":
                return message
            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
            received += body_len
            if received > self.max_content_size:
                raise HTTPException(
                    422,
                    detail={
                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
                        "code": 999,
                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
                    },
                )
            return message
        return inner

    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
            return
        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)

app = FastAPI()
app.add_middleware(ContentSizeLimitMiddleware, max_content_size=2**8)

client = TestClient(app)

def test_custom_middleware_exception(tmp_path: Path):
    # Use tmp_path for file operations
    path = tmp_path / "test.txt"
    path.write_bytes(b"x" * (2**16 + 1))
    
    with client:
        with open(path, "rb") as file:
            response = client.post("/middleware", files={"file": file})
        assert response.status_code == 422
```

## FASTAPI PROJECT TESTING SETUP - COMPREHENSIVE EXPLORATION REPORT

### 1. TEST FILES OVERVIEW

**Total test files:** 484 Python test files across the `/tests/` directory
**Total test functions:** 2,130 test functions
**Test Framework:** pytest 9.0.0+

**Main test directories:**
- `/tests/` - Primary test directory (root level)
- `/tests/test_request_params/` - Organized tests for Cookie, Form, Header, File, Body, and Path parameters
- `/tests/test_tutorial/` - Tests for tutorial code examples
- `/tests/benchmarks/` - Performance benchmarks
- `/scripts/tests/test_translation_fixer/` - Translation tool tests

### 2. CONFTEST.PY FILES

**conftest.py locations:**
- `/scripts/tests/test_translation_fixer/conftest.py` - Only dedicated conftest file found in the project

**Key content of translation_fixer conftest:**

```python
import shutil
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import pytest
from typer.testing import CliRunner

skip_on_windows = pytest.mark.skipif(
    sys.platform == "win32", reason="Skipping on Windows"
)

THIS_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.resolve()

def pytest_collection_modifyitems(config, items: list[pytest.Item]) -> None:
    if sys.platform != "win32":
        return
    for item in items:
        item_path = Path(item.fspath).resolve()
        if item_path.is_relative_to(THIS_DIR):
            item.add_marker(skip_on_windows)

@pytest.fixture(name="runner")
def get_runner():
    runner = CliRunner()
    with runner.isolated_filesystem():
        yield runner

@pytest.fixture(name="root_dir")
def prepare_paths(runner):
    docs_dir = Path("docs")
    en_docs_dir = docs_dir / "en" / "docs"
    lang_docs_dir = docs_dir / "lang" / "docs"
    en_docs_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    lang_docs_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    yield Path.cwd()

@pytest.fixture
def copy_test_files(root_dir: Path, request: pytest.FixtureRequest):
    en_file_path = Path(request.param[0])
    translation_file_path = Path(request.param[1])
    shutil.copy(str(en_file_path), str(root_dir / "docs" / "en" / "docs" / "doc.md"))
    shutil.copy(
        str(translation_file_path), str(root_dir / "docs" / "lang" / "docs" / "doc.md")
    )
```

**Note:** The main `/tests/` directory does NOT have a conftest.py file - tests use inline fixture definitions within individual test modules.

### 6. ACTUAL TEST FILE EXAMPLES

**Location:** `/tests/test_rate_limiter.py` (EXISTING RATE LIMITER TEST!)

```python
import time
import pytest
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from fastapi.middleware.rate_limit import RateLimitMiddleware

@pytest.fixture
def app():
    app = FastAPI()
    app.add_middleware(
        RateLimitMiddleware,
        requests_per_minute=60,
        burst_size=5,
    )

    @app.get("/")
    async def root():
        return {"message": "hello"}

    @app.get("/other")
    async def other():
        return {"message": "other"}

    return app

@pytest.fixture
def client(app):
    return TestClient(app)

class TestRateLimitMiddleware:
    def test_allows_requests_under_limit(self, client):
        """Requests under the burst size should succeed."""
        for _ in range(5):
            response = client.get("/")
            assert response.status_code == 200

    def test_blocks_requests_over_burst(self, client):
        """Requests exceeding burst size should be blocked."""
        # Use up the burst
        for _ in range(5):
            client.get("/")
        # Next request should be rate limited
        response = client.get("/")
        assert response.status_code == 429
        body = response.json()
        assert "Rate limit exceeded" in body["detail"]
        assert "retry_after" in body

    def test_rate_limit_headers_present(self, client):
        """All responses should include rate limit headers."""
        response = client.get("/")
        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
        assert "X-RateLimit-Remaining" in response.headers
        assert "X-RateLimit-Reset" in response.headers

    # ... more tests ...
```

from typing import Annotated
import pytest
from dirty_equals import IsOneOf
from fastapi import Cookie, FastAPI
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from inline_snapshot import snapshot
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

app = FastAPI()

@app.get("/required-str")
async def read_required_str(p: Annotated[str, Cookie()]):
    return {"p": p}

@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "path",
    ["/required-str"],
)
def test_required_str_schema(path: str):
    assert app.openapi()["paths"][path]["get"]["parameters"] == snapshot(
        [
            {
                "required": True,
                "schema": {"title": "P", "type": "string"},
                "name": "p",
                "in": "cookie",
            }
        ]
    )

def test_required_str_missing():
    client = TestClient(app)
    response = client.get("/required-str")
    assert response.status_code == 422
    assert response.json() == {
        "detail": [
            {
                "type": "missing",
                "loc": ["cookie", "p"],
                "msg": "Field required",
                "input": IsOneOf(None, {}),
            }
        ]
    }

def test_required_str():
    client = TestClient(app)
    client.cookies.set("p", "hello")
    response = client.get("/path")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json() == {"p": "hello"}
```

from typing import Any

def get_body_model_name(openapi: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> str:
    body = openapi["paths"][path]["post"]["requestBody"]
    body_schema = body["content"]["application/x-www-form-urlencoded"]["schema"]
    return body_schema.get("$ref", "").split("/")[-1]
```

### 8. MIDDLEWARE TEST PATTERNS

**Location:** `/tests/test_custom_middleware_exception.py`

```python
from pathlib import Path
from fastapi import APIRouter, FastAPI, File, UploadFile
from fastapi.exceptions import HTTPException
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
        self.app = app
        self.max_content_size = max_content_size

    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
        received = 0
        async def inner():
            nonlocal received
            message = await receive()
            if message["type"] != "http.request":
                return message
            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
            received += body_len
            if received > self.max_content_size:
                raise HTTPException(
                    422,
                    detail={
                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
                        "code": 999,
                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
                    },
                )
            return message
        return inner

    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
            return
        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)

app = FastAPI()
app.add_middleware(ContentSizeLimitMiddleware, max_content_size=2**8)

client = TestClient(app)

def test_custom_middleware_exception(tmp_path: Path):
    # Use tmp_path for file operations
    path = tmp_path / "test.txt"
    path.write_bytes(b"x" * (2**16 + 1))
    
    with client:
        with open(path, "rb") as file:
            response = client.post("/middleware", files={"file": file})
        assert response.status_code == 422
```

import time
import pytest
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from fastapi.middleware.rate_limit import RateLimitMiddleware


@pytest.fixture
def app():
    app = FastAPI()
    app.add_middleware(
        RateLimitMiddleware,
        requests_per_minute=60,
        burst_size=5,
    )

    @app.get("/")
    async def root():
        return {"message": "hello"}

    @app.get("/other")
    async def other():
        return {"message": "other"}

    return app


@pytest.fixture
def client(app):
    return TestClient(app)


class TestRateLimitMiddleware:
    def test_allows_requests_under_limit(self, client):
        """Requests under the burst size should succeed."""
        for _ in range(5):
            response = client.get("/")
            assert response.status_code == 200

    def test_blocks_requests_over_burst(self, client):
        """Requests exceeding burst size should be blocked."""
        # Use up the burst
        for _ in range(5):
            client.get("/")
        # Next request should be rate limited
        response = client.get("/")
        assert response.status_code == 429
        body = response.json()
        assert "Rate limit exceeded" in body["detail"]
        assert "retry_after" in body

    def test_rate_limit_headers_present(self, client):
        """All responses should include rate limit headers."""
        response = client.get("/")
        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
        assert "X-RateLimit-Remaining" in response.headers
        assert "X-RateLimit-Reset" in response.headers

    def test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases(self, client):
        """Remaining count should decrease with each request."""
        r1 = client.get("/")
        remaining1 = int(r1.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
        r2 = client.get("/")
        remaining2 = int(r2.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
        assert remaining2 < remaining1

    def test_rate_limit_per_ip(self, client):
        """Rate limits should be per-client IP."""
        # TestClient always uses the same IP, so this tests the basic flow
        response = client.get("/")
        assert response.status_code == 200
        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
        assert response.headers["X-RateLimit-Limit"] == "60"

    def test_different_paths_share_limit(self, client):
        """Rate limit applies across all paths for the same client."""
        # Use up burst across different endpoints
        for _ in range(3):
            client.get("/")
        for _ in range(2):
            client.get("/other")
        # Should be rate limited now
        response = client.get("/")
        assert response.status_code == 429

    def test_429_response_format(self, client):

            client.get("/")

        assert response.status_code == 429
        body = response.json()
        assert isinstance(body["detail"], str)
        assert isinstance(body["retry_after"], (int, float))
        assert body["retry_after"] > 0

    def test_custom_limits(self):
        """Should respect custom rate limit configuration."""
        app = FastAPI()
        app.add_middleware(
            RateLimitMiddleware,
            requests_per_minute=120,
            burst_size=2,
        )

        async def root():
            return {"ok": True}

        client = TestClient(app)
        # Burst of 2
        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
        assert client.get("/").status_code == 429

from contextlib import AsyncExitStack

from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send


# Used mainly to close files after the request is done, dependencies are closed
# in their own AsyncExitStack
class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
    def __init__(
        self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack"
    ) -> None:
        self.app = app
        self.context_name = context_name

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
            scope[self.context_name] = stack
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)

from pathlib import Path

from fastapi import APIRouter, FastAPI, File, UploadFile
from fastapi.exceptions import HTTPException
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

app = FastAPI()

router = APIRouter()


class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
    """Content size limiting middleware for ASGI applications
    Args:
      app (ASGI application): ASGI application
      max_content_size (optional): the maximum content size allowed in bytes, None for no limit
    """

    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
        self.app = app
        self.max_content_size = max_content_size

    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
        received = 0

        async def inner():
            nonlocal received
            message = await receive()
            if message["type"] != "http.request":
                return message  # pragma: no cover

            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
            received += body_len
            if received > self.max_content_size:
                raise HTTPException(
                    422,
                    detail={
                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
                        "code": 999,
                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
                    },
                )
            return message

        return inner

    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
            return

        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)


@router.post("/middleware")
def run_middleware(file: UploadFile = File(..., description="Big File")):
    return {"message": "OK"}


app.include_router(router)
app.add_middleware(ContentSizeLimitMiddleware, max_content_size=2**8)


client = TestClient(app)


def test_custom_middleware_exception(tmp_path: Path):
    default_pydantic_max_size = 2**16
    path = tmp_path / "test.txt"
    path.write_bytes(b"x" * (default_pydantic_max_size + 1))

    with client:
        with open(path, "rb") as file:
            response = client.post("/middleware", files={"file": file})
        assert response.status_code == 422, response.text
        assert response.json() == {
            "detail": {
                "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
                "code": 999,
                "message": "File limit exceeded",
            }
        }


def test_custom_middleware_exception_not_raised(tmp_path: Path):
    path = tmp_path / "test.txt"
    path.write_bytes(b"<file content>")

    with client:
        with open(path, "rb") as file:
            response = client.post("/middleware", files={"file": file})
        assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
        assert response.json() == {"message": "OK"}

input.prompt:
I need to design a rate limiting middleware for the FastAPI framework project (this is the FastAPI framework itself, not an app). Here's everything I've learned:

## Context
- **Project**: FastAPI framework (v0.135.3), built on Starlette
- **Middleware package**: `fastapi/middleware/` — contains thin wrappers. Most re-export Starlette middleware (cors.py, gzip.py, etc.). One custom middleware exists: `asyncexitstack.py`.
- **`__init__.py`**: Currently just `from starlette.middleware import Middleware as Middleware`
- **Existing tests**: `tests/test_rate_limiter.py` already exists with 8 tests that import from `fastapi.middleware.rate_limit` — this is the contract I must satisfy.

## Test Contract (from tests/test_rate_limiter.py)

The tests expect:
```python
from fastapi.middleware.rate_limit import RateLimitMiddleware

app.add_middleware(RateLimitMiddleware, requests_per_minute=60, burst_size=5)
```

Test requirements:
1. `test_allows_requests_under_limit` — 5 requests with burst_size=5 all return 200
2. `test_blocks_requests_over_burst` — 6th request returns 429, body has `detail` (contains "Rate limit exceeded") and `retry_after`
3. `test_rate_limit_headers_present` — All 200 responses have `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `X-RateLimit-Reset`
4. `test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases` — `X-RateLimit-Remaining` decreases between consecutive requests
5. `test_rate_limit_per_ip` — `X-RateLimit-Limit` header equals "60" (the requests_per_minute value)
6. `test_different_paths_share_limit` — Rate limit is shared across all paths for same client (3 to "/" + 2 to "/other" = 5, then 6th is 429)
7. `test_429_response_format` — 429 body: `detail` is str, `retry_after` is int/float > 0
8. `test_custom_limits` — `requests_per_minute=120, burst_size=2` works correctly

## Middleware Architecture Pattern

Custom ASGI middleware in this project follows this pattern (from asyncexitstack.py):
```python
class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = ...) -> None:
        self.app = app
        ...

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        ...
        await self.app(scope, receive, send)
```

The middleware is a pure ASGI middleware (not BaseHTTPMiddleware). It receives `(scope, receive, send)`.

## Token Bucket Algorithm Requirements

- **Per-client IP**: Each IP gets its own bucket
- **Configurable**: `requests_per_minute` (refill rate) and `burst_size` (max tokens)
- **Token bucket**: starts with `burst_size` tokens. Each request consumes 1 token. Tokens refill at `requests_per_minute / 60` tokens per second.
- When empty → 429 with retry_after (seconds until a token is available)

## Key Design Decisions Needed

1. **How to extract client IP from ASGI scope**: `scope["client"][0]` gives the IP
2. **Token storage**: Simple in-memory dict (this is framework-level middleware, no external deps)
3. **Thread safety**: Need to handle concurrent access. `asyncio.Lock` or simple dict access (Python GIL makes dict ops atomic for simple cases, but better to be safe)
4. **Response injection for headers**: Need to intercept `send` to add headers to HTTP response start messages
5. **429 response**: Must return JSON `{"detail": "Rate limit exceeded", "retry_after": <float>}`

## Files to Create/Modify

1. **CREATE**: `fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py` — The middleware implementation
2. **MODIFY**: `fastapi/middleware/__init__.py` — Export RateLimitMiddleware

Please design a detailed implementation plan including the token bucket class design, the ASGI middleware structure, how to inject response headers, how to handle the 429 case, and the exact exports needed.

content.text:
I have completed my thorough exploration of the codebase. Here is the full implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: RateLimitMiddleware for FastAPI

### Overview

Create a pure ASGI middleware at `fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py` implementing a token bucket rate limiter, per-client-IP, with configurable `requests_per_minute` and `burst_size`. The middleware must pass all 8 tests in `tests/test_rate_limiter.py`.

---

### 1. File: `fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py` (CREATE)

This is the core deliverable. It contains two classes and follows the existing `asyncexitstack.py` pattern (raw ASGI middleware, not `BaseHTTPMiddleware`).

#### 1a. `TokenBucket` class (internal, not exported)

```
class TokenBucket:
    __slots__ = ("rate", "max_tokens", "tokens", "last_refill")
```

**Fields:**
- `rate: float` -- tokens per second, computed as `requests_per_minute / 60.0`
- `max_tokens: int` -- the burst size (maximum tokens the bucket can hold)
- `tokens: float` -- current token count (starts at `max_tokens`)
- `last_refill: float` -- `time.monotonic()` timestamp of last refill

**Methods:**

- `__init__(self, rate: float, max_tokens: int) -> None` -- Initialize with `rate`, `max_tokens`, set `tokens = max_tokens`, `last_refill = time.monotonic()`.

- `consume(self) -> tuple[bool, float, int]` -- The core method. Returns `(allowed, retry_after, remaining)`.
  - Compute `now = time.monotonic()`
  - Compute elapsed time since `last_refill`
  - Refill: `self.tokens = min(self.max_tokens, self.tokens + elapsed * self.rate)`
  - Update `self.last_refill = now`
  - If `self.tokens >= 1.0`: deduct 1, compute `remaining = int(self.tokens)`, return `(True, 0.0, remaining)`
  - Else: compute `retry_after = (1.0 - self.tokens) / self.rate` (time until 1 token is available), return `(False, retry_after, 0)`

**Design rationale:** Using `time.monotonic()` (not `time.time()`) avoids issues with system clock changes. The `remaining` is cast to `int` because the test does `int(r1.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])`.

#### 1b. `RateLimitMiddleware` class (exported)

```python
class RateLimitMiddleware:
    def __init__(
        self,
        app: ASGIApp,
        requests_per_minute: int = 60,
        burst_size: int = 10,
    ) -> None:
```

**Fields:**
- `self.app: ASGIApp` -- the wrapped application
- `self.requests_per_minute: int` -- stored for the `X-RateLimit-Limit` header
- `self.burst_size: int` -- max tokens per bucket
- `self.rate: float` -- `requests_per_minute / 60.0`
- `self.buckets: dict[str, TokenBucket]` -- keyed by client IP string

**`__call__` method:**

```python
async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
```

**Logic flow:**

1. **Non-HTTP passthrough:** If `scope["type"] != "http"`, immediately delegate: `await self.app(scope, receive, send)` and return. This handles WebSocket, lifespan, etc.

2. **Extract client IP:** `client = scope.get("client")`. If `client` is not None, `client_ip = client[0]`. If `client` is None, use a fallback key like `"unknown"`.

3. **Get or create bucket:** Look up `client_ip` in `self.buckets`. If missing, create `TokenBucket(self.rate, self.burst_size)` and store it.

4. **Consume a token:** Call `bucket.consume()` getting `(allowed, retry_after, remaining)`.

5. **If NOT allowed (429 case):**
   - Build a JSON response body: `{"detail": "Rate limit exceeded", "retry_after": round(retry_after, 2)}` (rounding to 2 decimal places gives a clean float that is > 0).
   - Encode to bytes: `body = json.dumps(response_body).encode("utf-8")`
   - Send the ASGI `http.response.start` message with status 429, headers: `content-type: application/json`, `content-length: len(body)`, plus the three rate limit headers.
   - Send the ASGI `http.response.body` message with the body bytes.
   - Return (do NOT call `self.app`).

6. **If allowed (inject headers into normal response):**
   - Create a wrapper around `send` that intercepts the `http.response.start` message and injects the rate limit headers.
   - Call `await self.app(scope, receive, send_with_headers)`.

**Header injection detail (the `send` wrapper):**

```python
async def send_with_headers(message: Message) -> None:
    if message["type"] == "http.response.start":
        headers = MutableHeaders(scope=message)
        headers.append("X-RateLimit-Limit", str(self.requests_per_minute))
        headers.append("X-RateLimit-Remaining", str(remaining))
        headers.append("X-RateLimit-Reset", str(int(time.time()) + 60))
    await send(message)
```

Key details:
- Use `MutableHeaders` from `starlette.datastructures` to manipulate the headers list in the ASGI message. This is the same pattern used by Starlette's own `CORSMiddleware`.
- `X-RateLimit-Limit`: the `requests_per_minute` value as a string (test asserts `"60"`).
- `X-RateLimit-Remaining`: the `remaining` count from `consume()` as a string. The test asserts it can be parsed with `int()` and decreases between requests.
- `X-RateLimit-Reset`: a Unix timestamp (seconds) of when the rate limit window resets. Computed as `int(time.time()) + 60`. This is standard convention and the test only checks for its presence, not its value.

**Imports needed in `rate_limit.py`:**

```python
import json
import time
from typing import Any

from starlette.datastructures import MutableHeaders
from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Message, Receive, Scope, Send
```

**Why MutableHeaders?** The `message["headers"]` in ASGI is a list of `[name_bytes, value_bytes]` pairs. `MutableHeaders(scope=message)` wraps this list and provides `.append(name, value)` which handles byte encoding. This is the idiomatic Starlette approach -- `CORSMiddleware` uses the same technique.

#### 1c. Thread Safety Analysis

The tests use `TestClient` which runs synchronously in a single thread. In production, a single asyncio event loop handles all requests cooperatively. Since `TokenBucket.consume()` is a synchronous method with no `await` points, it will not be interrupted by another coroutine mid-execution. Therefore, no `asyncio.Lock` is needed -- the GIL + cooperative scheduling guarantee atomicity of the consume operation.

If this middleware were ever used with multiple workers (e.g., gunicorn with multiple processes), each worker would have its own in-memory bucket dict, which is acceptable and expected for in-process rate limiting.

#### 1d. Complete `__call__` pseudocode

```
async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
    if scope["type"] != "http":
        await self.app(scope, receive, send)
        return

    client = scope.get("client")
    client_ip = client[0] if client else "unknown"

    if client_ip not in self.buckets:
        self.buckets[client_ip] = TokenBucket(self.rate, self.burst_size)
    bucket = self.buckets[client_ip]

    allowed, retry_after, remaining = bucket.consume()

    if not allowed:
        body = json.dumps({
            "detail": "Rate limit exceeded",
            "retry_after": round(retry_after, 2),
        }).encode("utf-8")
        await send({
            "type": "http.response.start",
            "status": 429,
            "headers": [
                [b"content-type", b"application/json"],
                [b"content-length", str(len(body)).encode()],
                [b"x-ratelimit-limit", str(self.requests_per_minute).encode()],
                [b"x-ratelimit-remaining", b"0"],
                [b"x-ratelimit-reset", str(int(time.time()) + 60).encode()],
            ],
        })
        await send({
            "type": "http.response.body",
            "body": body,
        })
        return

    async def send_with_headers(message):
        if message["type"] == "http.response.start":
            headers = MutableHeaders(scope=message)
            headers.append("X-RateLimit-Limit", str(self.requests_per_minute))
            headers.append("X-RateLimit-Remaining", str(remaining))
            headers.append("X-RateLimit-Reset", str(int(time.time()) + 60))
        await send(message)

    await self.app(scope, receive, send_with_headers)
```

**Note on 429 headers:** For the 429 response, I construct the raw ASGI headers as byte pairs directly (no MutableHeaders needed since we build from scratch). The header names must be lowercase bytes per ASGI spec.

---

### 2. File: `fastapi/middleware/__init__.py` (MODIFY)

Currently contains only:
```python
from starlette.middleware import Middleware as Middleware
```

Add the import of `RateLimitMiddleware`:
```python
from starlette.middleware import Middleware as Middleware

from fastapi.middleware.rate_limit import RateLimitMiddleware as RateLimitMiddleware  # noqa
```

The `as RateLimitMiddleware` explicit re-export follows the pattern used by `cors.py`, `gzip.py`, etc. The `# noqa` comment suppresses unused-import linting, matching the convention in `cors.py`.

**Note:** The tests import from `fastapi.middleware.rate_limit` directly, not from `fastapi.middleware`. So the `__init__.py` export is technically not required for the tests to pass -- but it follows the project convention of making middleware accessible from the package namespace.

---

### 3. Test-by-Test Verification

Here is how each test maps to the implementation:

| Test | What it validates | Implementation detail |
|---|---|---|
| `test_allows_requests_under_limit` | 5 requests with `burst_size=5` all return 200 | Bucket starts with 5 tokens; each consume takes 1; all 5 succeed |
| `test_blocks_requests_over_burst` | 6th request returns 429 with correct body | After 5 tokens consumed, bucket is empty; returns JSON with `detail` and `retry_after` |
| `test_rate_limit_headers_present` | 200 has all 3 headers | `send_with_headers` injects them on `http.response.start` |
| `test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases` | `X-RateLimit-Remaining` decreases | First request: 4 remaining; second: 3 remaining (5 - 1 = 4, 4 - 1 = 3) |
| `test_rate_limit_per_ip` | `X-RateLimit-Limit` equals `"60"` | Header value is `str(self.requests_per_minute)` |
| `test_different_paths_share_limit` | Limit shared across paths | Bucket is keyed by IP only, not by path |
| `test_429_response_format` | `detail` is str, `retry_after` is int/float > 0 | `retry_after = round((1.0 - tokens) / rate, 2)` which is > 0 since tokens < 1.0 |
| `test_custom_limits` | `burst_size=2` works: 2 succeed, 3rd blocked | Different app instance with different config; bucket starts with 2 tokens |

**Critical edge case for `retry_after > 0`:** When the bucket is exactly at 0 tokens, `retry_after = (1.0 - 0.0) / rate = 1.0 / (120/60) = 0.5` seconds. Always positive. When tokens are between 0 and 1 (due to partial refill between rapid requests), `1.0 - tokens` is still positive, so `retry_after` is always > 0.

**Critical edge case for `remaining` type:** The test does `int(r1.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])`. Since we emit `str(remaining)` where `remaining = int(self.tokens)`, this is always a valid integer string.

---

### 4. Potential Pitfalls and Mitigations

1. **`time.monotonic()` vs `time.time()`:** Use `time.monotonic()` for token refill calculations (monotonic, not affected by clock adjustments). Use `time.time()` only for the `X-RateLimit-Reset` header which needs a wall-clock Unix timestamp.

2. **Memory growth from unbounded `self.buckets`:** For a framework-level middleware, this is acceptable. A production deployment would typically use a bounded cache or periodic cleanup, but that is an enhancement beyond the test contract. The dict will only grow with distinct client IPs, and for the test suite this is trivially 1 entry.

3. **`scope.get("client")` returning None:** This can happen in certain testing or proxy scenarios. Falling back to `"unknown"` ensures no crash. All requests without a client identifier share a single bucket, which is a reasonable degradation.

4. **Float precision in `retry_after`:** Using `round(retry_after, 2)` keeps the JSON output clean and ensures the value is a simple float, not something like `0.016666666666666666`.

5. **Header case sensitivity:** ASGI headers should be lowercase bytes. When building the 429 response manually, use lowercase (`b"x-ratelimit-limit"`). When using `MutableHeaders.append()`, it handles case normalization. The test reads headers via `response.headers["X-RateLimit-Limit"]` which is case-insensitive in httpx/requests.

---

### 5. Implementation Sequence

1. **Create `fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py`** with the `TokenBucket` class and `RateLimitMiddleware` class as described above.
2. **Modify `fastapi/middleware/__init__.py`** to add the re-export.
3. **Run `pytest tests/test_rate_limiter.py -v`** to verify all 8 tests pass.

No other files need modification. No new dependencies are introduced (only `json`, `time` from stdlib and `MutableHeaders`, types from starlette which is already a dependency).

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503370130-5vusaj/fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py` (CREATE - the entire middleware implementation)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503370130-5vusaj/fastapi/middleware/__init__.py` (MODIFY - add re-export)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503370130-5vusaj/tests/test_rate_limiter.py` (READ-ONLY reference - the test contract)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503370130-5vusaj/fastapi/middleware/asyncexitstack.py` (READ-ONLY reference - pattern for pure ASGI middleware)

tool_use_result.prompt:
I need to design a rate limiting middleware for the FastAPI framework project (this is the FastAPI framework itself, not an app). Here's everything I've learned:

## Context
- **Project**: FastAPI framework (v0.135.3), built on Starlette
- **Middleware package**: `fastapi/middleware/` — contains thin wrappers. Most re-export Starlette middleware (cors.py, gzip.py, etc.). One custom middleware exists: `asyncexitstack.py`.
- **`__init__.py`**: Currently just `from starlette.middleware import Middleware as Middleware`
- **Existing tests**: `tests/test_rate_limiter.py` already exists with 8 tests that import from `fastapi.middleware.rate_limit` — this is the contract I must satisfy.

## Test Contract (from tests/test_rate_limiter.py)

The tests expect:
```python
from fastapi.middleware.rate_limit import RateLimitMiddleware

app.add_middleware(RateLimitMiddleware, requests_per_minute=60, burst_size=5)
```

Test requirements:
1. `test_allows_requests_under_limit` — 5 requests with burst_size=5 all return 200
2. `test_blocks_requests_over_burst` — 6th request returns 429, body has `detail` (contains "Rate limit exceeded") and `retry_after`
3. `test_rate_limit_headers_present` — All 200 responses have `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `X-RateLimit-Reset`
4. `test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases` — `X-RateLimit-Remaining` decreases between consecutive requests
5. `test_rate_limit_per_ip` — `X-RateLimit-Limit` header equals "60" (the requests_per_minute value)
6. `test_different_paths_share_limit` — Rate limit is shared across all paths for same client (3 to "/" + 2 to "/other" = 5, then 6th is 429)
7. `test_429_response_format` — 429 body: `detail` is str, `retry_after` is int/float > 0
8. `test_custom_limits` — `requests_per_minute=120, burst_size=2` works correctly

## Middleware Architecture Pattern

Custom ASGI middleware in this project follows this pattern (from asyncexitstack.py):
```python
class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = ...) -> None:
        self.app = app
        ...

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        ...
        await self.app(scope, receive, send)
```

The middleware is a pure ASGI middleware (not BaseHTTPMiddleware). It receives `(scope, receive, send)`.

## Token Bucket Algorithm Requirements

- **Per-client IP**: Each IP gets its own bucket
- **Configurable**: `requests_per_minute` (refill rate) and `burst_size` (max tokens)
- **Token bucket**: starts with `burst_size` tokens. Each request consumes 1 token. Tokens refill at `requests_per_minute / 60` tokens per second.
- When empty → 429 with retry_after (seconds until a token is available)

## Key Design Decisions Needed

1. **How to extract client IP from ASGI scope**: `scope["client"][0]` gives the IP
2. **Token storage**: Simple in-memory dict (this is framework-level middleware, no external deps)
3. **Thread safety**: Need to handle concurrent access. `asyncio.Lock` or simple dict access (Python GIL makes dict ops atomic for simple cases, but better to be safe)
4. **Response injection for headers**: Need to intercept `send` to add headers to HTTP response start messages
5. **429 response**: Must return JSON `{"detail": "Rate limit exceeded", "retry_after": <float>}`

## Files to Create/Modify

1. **CREATE**: `fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py` — The middleware implementation
2. **MODIFY**: `fastapi/middleware/__init__.py` — Export RateLimitMiddleware

Please design a detailed implementation plan including the token bucket class design, the ASGI middleware structure, how to inject response headers, how to handle the 429 case, and the exact exports needed.
